text.title: Ops Dashboard Setup Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Ops Dashboard Setup. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: One dashboard, not many. Multi-pane defeats the purpose.
rule.rationale: Multi-pane setups guarantee the wrong pane gets opened.

rule.statement: ≤7 widgets per dashboard. More = scan-fatigue + ignored signal.
rule.rationale: Above 7 widgets the human stops scanning.

rule.statement: Each widget declares a freshness SLA (≤24h for ops, ≤1h for incident).
rule.rationale: Stale data drives wrong decisions silently.

rule.statement: Each widget has a named owner accountable for its definition + freshness.
Each widget declares a threshold + notification channel — value without alert = mural, not dashboard.
Monthly review verifies widgets still match decisions; widgets without decisions get removed.
rule.rationale: Without monthly cull the dashboard inflates back to noise.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ops-dashboard-setup.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
